H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: n=95 produced a strong‑confidence mean of 7.15 (std 1.78) with 66% positive outcomes. Qualitative (n=29) emphasizes a 10‑minute settlement and high music love (Loved 31%). YouTube reach is highest in the set (30 views) but lacks retention data. AutoGluon predicts 6.86 for overstimulation, indicating the observed mean exceeds expectations.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: A strong sense of calm emerged after around ten minutes, and listeners loved the piece.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: n=51, mean 6.92 (std 1.73), strong confidence, 65% positive. Qualitative feedback (n=21) reports 10‑minute settlement and balanced rhythmic perception. YouTube views (5) are low. AutoGluon prediction (6.86) aligns closely with the observed mean.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Users settled in about ten minutes and found the rhythm very soothing.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: n=47, mean 7.04 (std 1.68), meaningful confidence, 64% positive. Qualitative (n=23) highlights use for sleep and work, with balanced rhythm ratings. YouTube views (8) remain limited. AutoGluon’s 6.86 prediction is slightly below the observed mean.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners used it for sleep or work, feeling a calm effect after five‑ten minutes.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: The protocol collected n=67 responses with a mean of 7.22 (std 1.93), classified as strong confidence. Outcome distribution skews toward the upper decile, and 63% of listeners reported positive change. Qualitative data (n=23) emphasizes a 10‑minute settlement and high music approval (Liked/Loved 72%). YouTube reach is minimal (7 views, no retention), suggesting limited discovery. AutoGluon predicts a higher anxiety‑zone score of 7.84, indicating a modest positive delta that aligns with the observed efficacy.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners generally felt better after about ten minutes, with many saying they liked the music a lot.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: n=48 produced a mean of 6.94 (std 1.62) with meaningful confidence. Positive outcomes reached 67% of respondents. Qualitative feedback (n=20) notes faster 5‑minute settlement and mixed music sentiment (30% neutral). YouTube views are low (6) and lack retention data. AutoGluon’s anxiety prediction (7.84) exceeds the observed mean, hinting at untapped potential.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: People reported quick relief (around five minutes) and mostly liked the sound, though some were neutral.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: With n=36, the mean is 7.08 (std 1.89) and confidence remains meaningful. Positive response rate sits at 61%. Qualitative responses (n=20) highlight strong rhythmic calming (47% Very Settling) and a 10‑minute settlement mode. YouTube views (4) are negligible. AutoGluon forecasts 7.84 for anxiety, modestly above the observed score.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Most users felt a calming shift after ten minutes and praised the soothing rhythm.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: The crisis protocol gathered n=43, mean 6.35 (std 1.70), meaningful confidence. Positive outcomes were 53%. Qualitative insight (n=24) shows a rapid 5‑minute settlement and high music liking. YouTube views are low (6). AutoGluon predicts a crisis‑zone score of 7.96, indicating a sizable positive delta and suggesting the protocol may under‑perform relative to its design potential.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Even in crisis moments, listeners noticed modest improvement within five minutes and liked the track.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: n=33 yielded a mean of 6.52 (std 1.70) with meaningful confidence and 55% positive responses. Qualitative data (n=16) reports 5‑10 minute settlement and mixed music opinions. YouTube views (4) are minimal. AutoGluon’s crisis prediction of 7.96 exceeds the observed mean, again flagging possible under‑performance.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners in acute distress reported a small boost after five‑ten minutes and mixed feelings about the music.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: n=27, mean 6.52 (std 1.91), meaningful confidence, 52% positive. Qualitative (n=19) notes immediate settlement for 37% of listeners and balanced rhythm ratings. YouTube views (5) are low. AutoGluon predicts 7.96, a sizeable positive delta.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Many felt immediate change, with balanced rhythm perception and a modest uplift.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: n=54, mean 7.19 (std 1.86), strong confidence, 65% positive. Qualitative (n=15) shows a dominant 10‑minute settlement and strong music approval (Liked/Loved 86%). YouTube views (4) are minimal. AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for sleep, indicating the observed mean is modestly lower.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Most felt better after ten minutes, with high satisfaction for the music.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: n=45, mean 7.09 (std 1.55), meaningful confidence, 67% positive. Qualitative (n=15) notes extended settlement (up to 20 minutes) and balanced rhythm. YouTube views (5) are low. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, exceeding the observed mean by ≈0.45.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners reported lasting improvement, often after longer sessions, and liked the track.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: n=28, mean 7.07 (std 1.68), meaningful confidence, 64% positive. Qualitative (n=22) reports mixed settlement times and balanced rhythm. YouTube views (3) are negligible. AutoGluon’s 7.54 prediction suggests a positive delta.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: A steady calming effect was noted, with many appreciating the rhythm.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: n=41, mean 6.46 (std 1.82), meaningful confidence, 56% positive. Qualitative (n=23) reports a notable proportion of ‘Slightly busy’ rhythm perception, possibly indicating structural mismatch. YouTube views (5). AutoGluon predicts 7.73 for focus, a substantial positive delta (+1.27) signalling under‑performance.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus improvements were modest, and some felt the rhythm a bit busy.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: n=32, mean 7.19 (std 1.60), meaningful confidence, 66% positive. Qualitative (n=17) shows improved rhythm comfort and higher music love. YouTube views (4). AutoGluon predicts 7.73, a modest delta (+0.54).
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Noticeable focus gains were reported, with better rhythm comfort than L1.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: n=35, mean 7.63 (std 1.97), meaningful confidence, 71% positive. Qualitative (n=29) emphasizes strong improvement and balanced rhythm. YouTube views (15) are the highest among THRIVING protocols. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, aligning closely with the observed high mean.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: High focus and clarity gains were reported, with many loving the piece.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: n=34, mean 6.74 (std 1.73), meaningful confidence, 53% positive. Qualitative (n=21) shows mixed immediate and 10‑minute settlement, with moderate music liking. YouTube views (2) provide no retention signal. AutoGluon does not assign a specific prediction for Creative Flow, but its zone ranking places it mid‑range.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners experienced modest creative flow benefits, with mixed immediate and delayed effects.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: n=24, mean 6.79 (std 1.41), meaningful confidence, 58% positive. Qualitative (n=15) mentions higher music love (Loved 40%) and similar settlement. YouTube views (2).
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: A slightly stronger creative boost was reported, with many loving the music.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: n=18, mean 7.28 (std 1.32), developing confidence, 67% positive. Qualitative (n=25) highlights notable improvement and balanced rhythm. YouTube views (7). AutoGluon predicts a focus score of 7.73 for T‑FC‑L3, close to this protocol’s high performance, suggesting cross‑zone benefit.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: The strongest creative lift among its group, with listeners noting clear improvement.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: n=25, mean 7.04 (std 1.34), meaningful confidence, 64% positive. Qualitative (n=23) notes varied settlement times and neutral music sentiment. YouTube views (3).
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners felt balanced and steady after the session.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: n=22, mean 6.68 (std 2.03), meaningful confidence, 59% positive. Qualitative (n=16) shows the highest std in THRIVING, suggesting a bimodal listener mix. YouTube views (4).
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Responses varied widely, indicating mixed balance effects.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: n=14, mean 6.93 (std 1.73), developing confidence, 71% positive. Qualitative (n=19) records a non‑settle rate of 21%, the highest in the series. YouTube views (3). AutoGluon predicts 7.69 for maintenance, a modest positive delta (+0.76).
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Most reported good balance, though a few did not feel settled.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: n=39, mean 6.74 (std 1.94), meaningful confidence, 54% positive. Qualitative (n=22) highlights high music love (Loved 45%) and balanced rhythm. YouTube views (8). AutoGluon cross‑buckets this protocol across five zones, suggesting universal entrainment potential.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Mood and energy improved modestly, with many loving the track.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: n=25, mean 6.76 (std 1.71), meaningful confidence, 64% positive. Qualitative (n=15) reports significant improvement for many listeners. YouTube views (8). AutoGluon cross‑buckets similarly, reinforcing its generic utility.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Similar modest gains, with a few reporting significant improvement.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: n=18, mean 6.28 (std 1.74), developing confidence, 56% positive. Qualitative (n=25) notes 20% ‘No effect’ responses, the highest in the series. YouTube views (4). AutoGluon cross‑buckets, but observed mean lags behind the predicted universal score.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Mixed results, with a noticeable share reporting no effect.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: n=22, mean 6.36 (std 2.01), meaningful confidence, 45% positive. High std indicates heterogeneous response. Qualitative (n=17) notes modest improvement and balanced rhythm. YouTube views (5). AutoGluon predicts 7.88 for motivation, a strong positive delta (+1.52) indicating under‑performance.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation lift was modest and varied among listeners.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: n=14, mean 6.21 (std 1.63), developing confidence, 43% positive. Qualitative (n=21) reports long settlement (20‑minute mode). YouTube views (7). AutoGluon predicts 7.88, delta +1.67.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Motivation gains were low and settlement took longer.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: n=13, mean 7.15 (std 2.38), developing confidence, 62% positive. Distribution is wide, suggesting a bimodal effect. Qualitative (n=17) shows mixed rhythm and higher music love. YouTube views (7). AutoGluon predicts 7.88, a small negative delta (‑0.73). This protocol also shows the strongest cross‑run drift (sim 0.507).
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Some users reported a strong motivation boost, while others saw little change.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: n=24, mean 6.75 (std 1.62), meaningful confidence, 54% positive. Qualitative (n=25) reports strong music love (Loved 36%). YouTube views (8). AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for mind‑clarity, a positive delta (+0.79).
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners noticed a modest mind‑clarity boost and liked the music.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: n=16, mean 6.81 (std 1.64), developing confidence, 56% positive. Qualitative (n=20) shows high ‘Very settling’ rhythm. YouTube views (12). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, a delta of +0.73.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Slightly better clarity was reported, with many finding the rhythm very soothing.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: n=10, mean 5.70 (std 2.45), developing confidence, 50% positive. Distribution is clearly bimodal, with a spike at low scores, signalling a population split. Qualitative (n=17) reports mixed rhythm and a few negative effects. YouTube views (8). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, a large negative delta (‑1.84) reinforcing the need for redesign.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Results were mixed, with some users feeling little benefit and a few even worse.

sphere_commentary.overview:
Outcome scores across the three series indicate generally positive listener responses with moderate consistency.
• HEALING shows the highest sample size (n=574) and tightest variance, supporting its role in nervous system regulation.
• THRIVING delivers strong scores, especially in Focus & Clarity, though some protocols display higher variability.
• TRANSFORMING exhibits the expected spread for low‑affect, ascending‑arc designs, with a few outliers warranting closer look.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Each series clusters around mid‑range efficacy, reflecting their distinct design intents.
• HEALING protocols average ~7.0 with the lowest standard deviations, indicating reliable regulation effects.
• THRIVING holds comparable means but shows a gradient from lower (Focus L1) to higher (Focus L3) scores, suggesting benefit from longer hold phases.
• TRANSFORMING spans a broader range (~6.2–7.0), consistent with its ascending‑arc approach for mood and motivation states.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols deviate noticeably from series‑wide patterns, suggesting potential mismatches or data issues.
• X-MC-L3 (mean 5.7, std 2.45, n 10) shows a low mean and high dispersion, hinting at a bimodal response.
• T-FC-L1 (mean 6.46, std 1.82, n 41) underperforms relative to its peers and AutoGluon’s higher predicted score.
• X-MD-L3 (drift sim 0.507) exhibits the strongest cross‑run drift, indicating possible population or routing changes.
These anomalies suggest further subgroup analysis and protocol refinement are needed.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted actions could tighten efficacy and clarify usage patterns.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 — redesign audio‑frequency envelope or test L2‑style parameters to address low mean and bimodality.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L1 — augment hold duration or increase rhythmic complexity to lift its mean toward the predicted 7.7.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-OS-L1 — boost YouTube promotion to leverage its strong outcome score and improve audience reach.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L3 — replicate its structural parameters (e.g., longer hold, richer phi‑sigmoid) across lower‑scoring focus protocols.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-ME-L1 — explore cross‑zone applicability, possibly creating a universal variant for mood, mind clarity, and low‑affect states.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding drift and clustering reveal evolving analytic signals across the library.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: 29 of 30 protocols show similarity <0.85, indicating genuine pattern evolution rather than noise.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Expected pairings (e.g., H‑SL‑L1↔H‑SL‑L2, T‑FC‑L1↔T‑FC‑L2) are tight; L3 variants drift away, matching observed performance gaps.
• Confidence note: drift metrics are based on two time points (April‑June 2026) and are reliable for flagging protocols requiring re‑evaluation.
